🚚 Sysco · Foodservice Distribution

Smart Assignment

An agentic workflow that recommends the best delivery day & time slot for a new customer — enforcing hard operational rules in code, ranking the survivors on weighted business factors, and escalating to a human when the call is close.

✅ Deterministic, auditable decisions 🤖 LLM-written reasoning 🧪 Running on mock data
The problem

Onboarding a new account, every time, the same way

When a new foodservice customer signs on, someone has to decide which delivery route and time window they should join. That decision balances truck capacity, geography, and the customer's preference — and it needs to be consistent, explainable, and fast. Smart Assignment makes that call automatically, and flags the tricky ones for a specialist instead of guessing.

How it works

Five steps, from intake to recommendation

The customer's details go in one end; a fully-explained slot recommendation (or a human escalation) comes out the other.

1

Intake

Capture the customer's address, order quantity (cases), and preferred delivery window (optional).

2

Geo-Lookup

Geocode the address and pick the Top-3 nearest candidate routes by proximity.

3

Constraint Check

Drop any route that fails a hard rule — serviceability, capacity, or window.

4

Score & Rank

Rank the survivors with weighted scoring across clustering, capacity buffer, and window fit.

5

Recommend

Return the top slot with a reasoning trace — or escalate to a human specialist.

The rules it enforces

Hard constraints — non-negotiable, checked in code

These are objective facts, not judgment calls. A route that fails any of them is removed before ranking — the agent can never "reason" a customer onto a full truck or outside the serviceable area.

📍

Geographic serviceability

The customer must fall within the route's serviceable radius.

📦

Route capacity

The truck stays at or below 90% capacity after adding this order.

🕑

Delivery-window fit

The route offers a window overlapping the customer's preference, if stated.

How it ranks the rest

Weighted scoring factors

Among the routes that pass every hard rule, these weighted factors decide the winner. Weights reflect priority and are fully configurable.

🧭

Geographic clustering

How tightly the customer clusters with the route's existing stops — tighter clusters mean more predictable arrivals.

weight 0.45
🛡️

Capacity buffer

How much headroom is left after the add — more buffer means a route that's resilient to day-of disruption.

weight 0.30
🎯

Window match

How well the route's window covers the customer's stated preference.

weight 0.25
The outcome

Three possible decisions

Every run ends in one of three states. Anything below a 70% confidence threshold, or with no valid slot at all, goes to a human — with full context attached.

✔ Recommended

A clear winner above the confidence threshold — ready to auto-assign.

⚠ Low confidence

A slot is proposed, but the options are close — a specialist confirms before committing.

✖ No feasible slot

Every candidate failed a hard rule — routed to a specialist for a manual decision.

See it in action

Live examples on mock Sysco data

These cards are generated straight from the workflow's output. Each customer lands on a different outcome; expand “Routes evaluated” to audit the full decision.

067-100001

Bayou City Bistro

1200 McKinney St, Houston, TX 77010
90 cases prefers 07:00–10:00 3 routes evaluated
✔ Recommended confidence 89%

→ RTE-4100 · Central Houston · TUE · 07:00–10:00 (score 0.77)

Geographic clustering
0.94
Capacity buffer
0.32
Window match
1.00
Why this slotRecommending RTE-4100 (Central Houston) on TUE, window 07:00-10:00, score 0.77. Key factors: geographic_clustering (avg 0.8 mi to existing stops); window_match (180/180 min of preferred window covered); capacity_buffer (290 cases headroom (68% utilized post-add)). Confidence 89%.
Routes evaluated (3)
RTE-4100 · Central Houston · TUE · 0.1 miFEASIBLE · 0.77
  • ✓ serviceability: 0.1 mi from route center (limit 12.0 mi)
  • ✓ capacity: utilization 68% post-add (limit 90%), 290 cases headroom
  • ✓ window: 180 min overlap with preferred window (best route window 07:00-10:00)
RTE-4200 · West Houston / Energy Corridor · WED · 14.7 miINFEASIBLE
  • ✗ serviceability: 14.7 mi from route center (limit 12.0 mi)
  • ✓ capacity: utilization 52% post-add (limit 90%), 460 cases headroom
  • ✓ window: 150 min overlap with preferred window (best route window 07:30-11:00)
RTE-4400 · Southwest / Sugar Land · TUE · 18.7 miINFEASIBLE
  • ✗ serviceability: 18.7 mi from route center (limit 12.0 mi)
  • ✗ capacity: utilization 101% post-add (limit 90%), -10 cases headroom
  • ✓ window: 120 min overlap with preferred window (best route window 06:00-09:00)
067-100002

Galleria Grill & Catering

5085 Westheimer Rd, Houston, TX 77056
140 cases no window preference 3 routes evaluated
⚠ Low confidence — human review confidence 51%
below the confidence threshold

→ proposed: RTE-4200 · West Houston / Energy Corridor · WED · 07:30–11:00 (score 0.58)

Geographic clustering
0.67
Capacity buffer
0.43
Window match
0.60
Why it escalatedRecommending RTE-4200 (West Houston / Energy Corridor) on WED, window 07:30-11:00, score 0.58. Key factors: geographic_clustering (avg 5.0 mi to existing stops); window_match (no stated preference (neutral score)); capacity_buffer (410 cases headroom (57% utilized post-add)). Passed over: RTE-4100/TUE scored 0.52 (−0.06). Confidence 51% is below the 70% threshold (options are close / scores modest) — flagging for human review before committing.
Routes evaluated (3)
RTE-4100 · Central Houston · TUE · 5.9 miFEASIBLE · 0.52
  • ✓ serviceability: 5.9 mi from route center (limit 12.0 mi)
  • ✓ capacity: utilization 73% post-add (limit 90%), 240 cases headroom
  • ✓ window: no stated preference; any available window acceptable
RTE-4200 · West Houston / Energy Corridor · WED · 9.3 miFEASIBLE · 0.58
  • ✓ serviceability: 9.3 mi from route center (limit 12.0 mi)
  • ✓ capacity: utilization 57% post-add (limit 90%), 410 cases headroom
  • ✓ window: no stated preference; any available window acceptable
RTE-4400 · Southwest / Sugar Land · TUE · 13.3 miINFEASIBLE
  • ✗ serviceability: 13.3 mi from route center (limit 12.0 mi)
  • ✗ capacity: utilization 109% post-add (limit 90%), -60 cases headroom
  • ✓ window: no stated preference; any available window acceptable
067-100003

Katy Prairie Steakhouse

24600 Katy Fwy, Katy, TX 77494
260 cases prefers 06:00–08:00 3 routes evaluated
✖ No feasible slot — specialist confidence 0%
no candidate passed the hard rules
Why it escalatedNo feasible slot for Katy Prairie Steakhouse: every candidate route was ruled out by a hard constraint [RTE-4200 (WED): failed geographic_serviceability; RTE-4400 (TUE): failed geographic_serviceability, route_capacity; RTE-4100 (TUE): failed geographic_serviceability]. Escalating to a routing specialist for a manual decision (new route, schedule change, or capacity reallocation).
Routes evaluated (3)
RTE-4200 · West Houston / Energy Corridor · WED · 12.8 miINFEASIBLE
  • ✗ serviceability: 12.8 mi from route center (limit 12.0 mi)
  • ✓ capacity: utilization 69% post-add (limit 90%), 290 cases headroom
  • ✓ window: 30 min overlap with preferred window (best route window 07:30-11:00)
RTE-4400 · Southwest / Sugar Land · TUE · 16.0 miINFEASIBLE
  • ✗ serviceability: 16.0 mi from route center (limit 12.0 mi)
  • ✗ capacity: utilization 126% post-add (limit 90%), -180 cases headroom
  • ✓ window: 120 min overlap with preferred window (best route window 06:00-09:00)
RTE-4100 · Central Houston · TUE · 27.4 miINFEASIBLE
  • ✗ serviceability: 27.4 mi from route center (limit 12.0 mi)
  • ✓ capacity: utilization 87% post-add (limit 90%), 120 cases headroom
  • ✓ window: 60 min overlap with preferred window (best route window 07:00-10:00)
067-100004

Woodlands Fresh Cafe

1201 Lake Woodlands Dr, The Woodlands, TX 77380
150 cases prefers 09:00–12:00 3 routes evaluated
✔ Recommended confidence 93%

→ RTE-4300 · North Houston / The Woodlands · THU · 08:00–12:00 (score 0.85)

Geographic clustering
0.96
Capacity buffer
0.56
Window match
1.00
Why this slotRecommending RTE-4300 (North Houston / The Woodlands) on THU, window 08:00-12:00, score 0.85. Key factors: geographic_clustering (avg 0.6 mi to existing stops); window_match (180/180 min of preferred window covered); capacity_buffer (450 cases headroom (44% utilized post-add)). Confidence 93%.
Routes evaluated (3)
RTE-4300 · North Houston / The Woodlands · THU · 0.3 miFEASIBLE · 0.85
  • ✓ serviceability: 0.3 mi from route center (limit 16.0 mi)
  • ✓ capacity: utilization 44% post-add (limit 90%), 450 cases headroom
  • ✓ window: 180 min overlap with preferred window (best route window 08:00-12:00)
RTE-4200 · West Houston / Energy Corridor · WED · 27.7 miINFEASIBLE
  • ✗ serviceability: 27.7 mi from route center (limit 12.0 mi)
  • ✓ capacity: utilization 58% post-add (limit 90%), 400 cases headroom
  • ✓ window: 120 min overlap with preferred window (best route window 07:30-11:00)
RTE-4100 · Central Houston · TUE · 28.4 miINFEASIBLE
  • ✗ serviceability: 28.4 mi from route center (limit 12.0 mi)
  • ✓ capacity: utilization 74% post-add (limit 90%), 230 cases headroom
  • ✓ window: 90 min overlap with preferred window (best route window 10:30-12:30)
About this data. These examples run on mock Houston-area routes and geocoding so the workflow can be demonstrated end-to-end. Capacities, service radii, scoring weights, and thresholds are illustrative starting points — not validated Sysco policy. The route source and geocoder are designed to be swapped for real systems without changing the decision logic.
Smart Assignment · delivery slot recommendation for new customers · built on Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK).
Source & docs on GitHub. Decisions are deterministic and auditable; reasoning narration is LLM-written with a deterministic fallback.
